concerns.description: Significant text omission within a section
concerns.analysis: Equation 1 on page 7 is completely missing from the output text. The formula has been incorrectly replaced by a blank image placeholder with the description 'A solid white rectangle', leading to a significant loss of technical content.
concerns.location: Page 7, below 'Equation 1: Total Mass Loading at Outfall 002'

concerns.analysis: The source document contains a highly meaningful full-page aerial map on page 15 (Figure 1: Outfall 002 Sampling Locations) and a detailed flowchart diagram on page 16 (Figure 2: Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at Outfall 002). The map on page 15 is entirely missing from the HTML output (only a small scale bar image and the legend text appear). The flowchart on page 16 is also missing as an image and was instead improperly extracted as a bracketed text placeholder `[Image
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 15 features a large aerial map with annotations like 'River Water Intake at Facility' and page 16 features a diagram mapping out a flowchart process.
concerns.output_quote: The map image is absent from the HTML output. The flowchart is replaced by a text placeholder `[Image: A flowchart detailing...]` instead of an actual `` tag.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: The Table of Contents retains dotted leaders and hardcoded page numbers from the source PDF. As per the instructions, retaining source TOC page numbers and dotted leaders in the HTML output is a structural defect because the page references become meaningless.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis:
caption_as_header=2. Examples: p15: "Outfall 002 Sampling Locations
Chemours Fayetteville Works, "; p17: "Flowchart for Determining the Concentration for Loading at t". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

concerns.description: Missing images: 6 of 14 source image(s) not present in output
concerns.analysis: image_count_source=14, html_img_count=8, image_count_ratio=1.3571428571428572. Some source images are missing from the output.

concerns.description: Degraded image quality: 3 image(s) used fallback rendering instead of direct extraction
concerns.analysis: fallback_rendered_images=3. Examples: p7: "A solid white rectangle."; p15: "A thin horizontal line segment with tiny vertical "; p17: "A flowchart titled "Flowchart for Determining the ". These images were not directly extracted from the PDF — instead, an approximate page region was rendered as a PNG. Fallback images may be cropped incorrectly, show the wrong content, or miss meaningful visual elements.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (43): DocuSign Envelope ID: 1B477D8C-F5AD-4BCC-B766-EF28605DC046; Geosyntec Consultants of NC, P.C.
NC License No.: C-3500 and; Outfall 002 Mass Load
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=43, pages_affected=15/17. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=0, no_th_tables=2. Tables use <th> for data values, have a data row mismarked as a header row, or lack <th> elements entirely. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Incomplete image alt text: 15 image(s) with inadequate descriptions (15 truncated mid-sentence) — e.g. p1: "...ext and a stylized green, blue, and tan triangular"; p1: "... for Adrienne D. Nemura, displaying license number"; p1: "...ext "DocuSigned by:" above a handwritten signature"
concerns.analysis: incomplete_alt_text=15 (truncated=15, placeholder=0, empty=0). Per WCAG 2.1 AA, meaningful images must have descriptive alternative text for screen-reader accessibility.

Subsections 3.1 through 3.6 feature underlined headings in the source PDF. This underlining emphasis is not preserved in the output JSON, which renders them as standard plain-text headings without underline formatting.

concerns.description: Table column count mismatch: p16(source:4cols,output:3cols), p17(source:4cols,output:3cols)
concerns.analysis: Matched tables have different column counts. Pages: p16(source:4cols,output:3cols), p17(source:4cols,output:3cols)

concerns.description: Degenerate images detected: 2 images with extreme aspect ratio or tiny dimensions
Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "- HFPO-DA and PFHpA are also analyzed by EPA Metho". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.
